title: CFTC intervention in Michigan trades
summary: The CFTC directed Kalshi to continue fulfilling open Michigan trades, creating a practical conflict with a state-court position.
commercial_read: The federal-state dispute now affects the treatment of live contracts, not only licensing and market access.
content_angle: Kalshi appears caught between two authorities giving it incompatible instructions.
next_action: Lead editorial coverage and prepare formal invitations through official channels.

title: House hearing on sports prediction markets
summary: The House Agriculture Subcommittee scheduled a hearing on customer protections and market integrity in sports-event prediction markets.
commercial_read: The hearing should produce named policy positions, witness leads and concrete reform proposals.
content_angle: Five questions Congress should ask before deciding whether sports event contracts are financial products, gambling products or something new.
next_action: Run pre-hearing, live-monitoring and post-hearing workflows.

title: France blocks Polymarket
summary: French authorities ordered internet service providers to block access to Polymarket.
commercial_read: International expansion is becoming a country-by-country operational problem involving access, advertising, payments and classification.
content_angle: Can a prediction market build a global product when even the display of prices is treated differently across jurisdictions?
next_action: Build a European jurisdiction map and identify relevant speakers.

title: Malta explores bespoke prediction-market framework
summary: Malta is exploring a dedicated licensing approach outside conventional financial-services and gambling classifications.
commercial_read: European jurisdictions may compete to attract prediction-market operators and infrastructure.
content_angle: Europe may be splitting between jurisdictions that block the category and those trying to invent a regulatory home for it.
next_action: Confirm framework status and identify policy contacts.

title: North Carolina tax-without-licensing model
summary: North Carolina enacted a 6% tax on prediction-market net trading-fee revenue while recognising federal oversight.
commercial_read: States may accept federal regulation, avoid duplicate licensing and still collect revenue.
content_angle: North Carolina may have found the politically useful middle ground.
next_action: Map other states considering similar approaches.

title: Jump Trading expands prediction-markets team
summary: Jump reportedly doubled its prediction-markets team to roughly 20 people and is continuing to hire.
commercial_read: Prediction markets are becoming a genuine institutional trading strategy.
content_angle: The talent signal matters more than another volume record.
next_action: Approach Simon Johansen with a focused institutional-market-structure invitation.

title: Moreton builds dedicated prediction-markets strategy
summary: Moreton is hiring a senior Portfolio Manager to lead a strategy using quantitative modelling, agentic AI and domain expertise.
commercial_read: Dedicated capital and leadership are entering the category.
content_angle: Institutional interest is moving from exploration to team-building and capital allocation.
next_action: Map the team and identify the strategy lead.

title: Pascal raises for a trader-first venue
summary: Pascal announced a $9 million Series A led by Union Square Ventures and is building professional interfaces, APIs and OTC capability.
commercial_read: Professional traders may demand materially different venues from consumer-first prediction platforms.
content_angle: The next platform battle may be about who professional traders trust with size.
next_action: Contact both founders with differentiated session ideas.

title: DraftKings and UDX internalise exchange infrastructure
summary: Sports operators are moving toward proprietary exchanges, direct contract certification and market-maker relationships.
commercial_read: Owning distribution, brokerage, exchange technology and economics changes the competitive structure.
content_angle: Prediction markets are becoming an infrastructure contest.
next_action: Identify the executives responsible for exchange operations and liquidity.

title: Blockchain.com integrates Polymarket
summary: Blockchain.com embedded Polymarket access into its brokerage interface for eligible users.
commercial_read: Prediction-market distribution is moving into existing brokerage and crypto products.
content_angle: The exchange may matter less than the interface through which the customer first encounters it.
next_action: Contact integration and brokerage leadership.

title: Kalshi and AppliedXL launch biopharma markets
summary: A pilot covers late-stage clinical trials and FDA decisions with AppliedXL supporting resolution infrastructure.
commercial_read: Health markets create information value but also complex MNPI, ethical and restricted-person risks.
content_angle: Where does the insider perimeter end in a clinical trial?
next_action: Invite Francesco Marconi for an exploratory conversation.

title: Entertainment markets scale alongside leakage risk
summary: Kalshi entertainment volume reportedly exceeded $600 million in 2026 while reality-TV markets generated spoiler and informed-trading concerns.
commercial_read: Entertainment may be a powerful acquisition vertical and a serious brand-risk problem at the same time.
content_angle: Rights holders need prediction-market policies before prices create a public narrative for them.
next_action: Develop an entertainment-integrity session and recruit production-side voices.

title: Five-minute Bitcoin markets show settlement manipulation
summary: Research found abnormal reference-market order flow immediately before settlement of five-minute Polymarket Bitcoin contracts, followed by partial reversal.
commercial_read: Contract design can make manipulation of the underlying reference market economically rational.
content_angle: A prediction market may be manipulated without trading directly on it.
next_action: Contact the authors and develop a technical integrity session.

title: SEC boundary becomes commercially relevant
summary: Corporate-event contracts may raise securities-law or security-based-swap questions alongside CFTC oversight.
commercial_read: The next major jurisdictional problem may involve federal overlap as well as state conflict.
content_angle: When does an event contract become a securities product?
next_action: Recruit legal experts and map product types most exposed to overlap.

title: Restricted-person identification becomes infrastructure
summary: Robinhood surveillance leadership described manual identification of occupations, employers and connections that may create restricted access.
commercial_read: Basic KYC cannot identify every potential insider across sports, entertainment, biotech, weather and corporate events.
content_angle: Who is an insider when every contract has a different insider list?
next_action: Contact Robinhood, IC360 and Eventus.

title: FlightAware conflict exposes settlement data-rights risk
summary: FlightAware parent RTX said it had not authorised use of its data for proposed Kalshi flight-cancellation contracts.
commercial_read: Data licensing, correction policies, uptime and audit rights can determine whether a contract is launchable.
content_angle: You cannot settle what you do not own or license.
next_action: Map settlement-data dependencies and contact providers.

title: PMXT builds a unified prediction-market API
summary: PMXT is developing a CCXT-style interface with Python, TypeScript and MCP support across several venues.
commercial_read: Cross-venue standardisation becomes valuable as professional firms and autonomous agents expand.
content_angle: The prediction-market equivalent of CCXT may look like plumbing, which is exactly why it matters.
next_action: Validate production use and contact the founders.

title: OpenThomas trades weather markets autonomously
summary: OpenThomas is an open-source weather-market agent using multiple forecast models, bias adjustments and risk controls.
commercial_read: Weather is a plausible early laboratory for autonomous agents because settlement is frequent and objective.
content_angle: AI-agent claims should be judged after fees, spread, slippage and live execution.
next_action: Validate live performance before promoting commercial significance.

title: Augur pivots toward shared resolution infrastructure
summary: Augur Lituus proposes a decentralised resolution layer that could serve multiple prediction-market applications.
commercial_read: Resolution may become a shared infrastructure layer rather than a feature owned by each consumer venue.
content_angle: Augur may have concluded that the valuable layer is resolution, not the front end.
next_action: Research contributors and current development status.

title: Entain names prediction markets as competitive pressure
summary: Entain is cutting costs amid tax pressure and has also cited prediction markets among competitive pressures.
commercial_read: Prediction markets have become a named strategic consideration for a major gambling incumbent.
content_angle: The clearest sign the category matters may be incumbents mentioning it in restructuring discussions.
next_action: Identify relevant Entain executives and avoid claiming PMs caused the redundancies.

title: Eilers & Krejcik releases July market monitor
summary: The July monitor compares prediction-market and online sports-betting activity using trade-level data across multiple venues.
commercial_read: Reliable market monitoring is a strategic asset for content, sponsorship and programming.
content_angle: The category needs shared metrics and definitions before every volume claim becomes marketing.
next_action: Pursue a relationship and potential data collaboration.

title: USD.AI contract exposes resolution-design ambiguity
summary: A high-volume Polymarket token-valuation contract faced questions about launch timing, price source and valuation measurement.
commercial_read: Observable prices do not prevent disputes when contract definitions are weak.
content_angle: Resolution risk often begins in the wording, not the vote at the end.
next_action: Monitor final resolution and document the failure modes.
